summarization.a: The chart displays SIMS depth profiles comparing the intensity at various depths for isotopes of titanium (Ti-48), lithium (Li-6), and lanthanum (La-138) for films grown using one lithium precursor sub-cycle and three lithium sub-cycles.

a.question: Which film is thicker?
a.answer: The one grown with 3 lithium sub-cycles.

a.question: Is lithium incorporation in the bulk of the film uniform after 3 subcycles? What supports this conclusion?
a.answer: No.  Between 60 nm and 120 nm there is a broad surge in intensity. While the upper region (10–60 nm) overlaps with the 1-sub-cycle reference, suggesting normal growth, the massive surge in intensity near the bottom might indicate that excess lithium from the additional pulses migrated and segregated at the interface.

a.question: Which film has more uniform distribution of lithium?
a.answer: The film grown with one lithium precursor pulse.

a.question: Considering the film grown with one precursor pulse, is lanthanum concentration higher in the bulk or at the interface?
a.answer: At the interface, as indicated by the surge in intensity at approx. 80 nm.
